theme.strategy: Dual-mode (Light and Dark) with 'Editorial Future' aesthetic. Light mode should feel like a premium printed newspaper (e.g., Financial Times, off-white newsprint). Dark mode should feel like an exclusive executive club or Bloomberg Terminal (midnight ink).
theme.border_radius: Sharp and structured. Use `rounded-none` or max `rounded-sm` for a serious, authoritative feel. Avoid bubbly or highly rounded corners.
theme.shadows: Minimal. Rely on 1px borders for structure rather than floating drop-shadows.

rules.headlines: Use `font-serif` (Playfair Display) with `tracking-tight` and `leading-none`. Weights: Use `font-black` for massive heroes, `font-medium` for standard articles.
rules.body: Use `font-serif` (Source Serif Pro) with `leading-relaxed` and `text-lg` or `text-xl` for long-form reading comfort.
rules.ui_elements: Use `font-sans` (IBM Plex Sans) for datelines, author names, category tags, and data tables. Must be `uppercase`, `tracking-widest` (`tracking-[0.2em]`), and `text-xs`.

rules.primary: Elevated from the original bright blue (#2B5DAC) to a deeper, more institutional 'Oxford Navy' (#1A365D) for light mode. It projects financial stability and trust.
rules.accent: Elevated from generic orange (#FF5722) to 'Burnt Copper' (#D35400). Use sparingly—only for subscribe buttons, active states, or critical highlights.
rules.background: Light mode MUST use an 'off-white / newsprint' hex (#F9F8F6) to reduce eye strain and emulate high-end paper.

layouts.homepage: The 'Swiss Grid' editorial layout. Use asymmetric grids (e.g., `grid-cols-12`). The hero article spans 8 columns, while a 'Trending' list spans 4 columns. Separate sections with hard 1px horizontal lines (`border-t border-border/40`).
layouts.article_page: Single-column reading view. Max-width `max-w-3xl` (`prose prose-lg`) centered, with generous padding. Floating, sticky side-actions (share, save, progress bar).
layouts.spacing: Generous. Use `gap-8` to `gap-16` between major grid items. Use `py-24` for section separators.

components.article_cards: Cardless approach. Do not use standard Shadcn bordered cards with shadows. Render the image, category label, headline, and excerpt cleanly on the background, separated by a bottom border.
components.buttons: Sharp edges (`rounded-none`). Solid fill for primary (Subscribe), minimal wireframe outline (`border border-primary bg-transparent hover:bg-primary/5`) for secondary.
components.navigation: Sticky, Glassmorphism Header. High-end glass: `bg-background/80 backdrop-blur-xl border-b border-border`. DO NOT use completely transparent backgrounds.
components.newsletter: Minimalist form. 'Burnt Copper' submit button beside a clean `border-b` only input field (no full box).

visual_enhancers.image_treatment: Apply a subtle duotone or high-contrast filter to editorial images to unify the aesthetic. E.g., `grayscale hover:grayscale-0 transition-all duration-500`.
visual_enhancers.textures: Subtle grain or noise overlay fixed in the background (`opacity-5`) to make the digital screen feel like a physical publication.
visual_enhancers.micro_interactions: Images scale up slightly on hover within a hidden overflow container (`overflow-hidden`). Links feature a 1px expanding underline.

images.description: Wide-angle view of an iconic cricket stadium empty before a match
Businessman examines finance report on tablet
Cricket match is being played in a full stadium

accessibility.contrast: Ensure all text on image overlays uses a text-shadow or a dark gradient scrim (`bg-gradient-to-t from-black/80 to-transparent`) to meet APCA contrast standards.
accessibility.focus: All interactive elements must have a clear `focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-accent` state.
accessibility.testing: All interactive and key informational elements MUST include a `data-testid` attribute (e.g., `data-testid='subscribe-hero-button'`).

instructions_to_main_agent: Install required fonts from Google Fonts: Playfair Display, Source Serif Pro, IBM Plex Sans.
Install phosphor-icons (`yarn add @phosphor-icons/react`) for sharp, editorial-style duotone icons.
Avoid Shadcn 'Card' wrappers for news articles. Use raw HTML/Tailwind to build grid-aligned, border-separated content blocks.
Set global `selection:bg-accent selection:text-white` for a premium highlight effect.
Ensure dark/light mode toggle works flawlessly across all custom border and background utility classes.
UNIVERSAL_GUIDELINES_FOR_MAIN_AGENT: You tend to converge toward generic, 'on distribution' outputs. In frontend design, this creates what users call the 'AI slop' aesthetic. Avoid this: make creative, distinctive frontends that surprise and delight.
Dark colors look good when used independently without gradients. DO NOT use dark colors as gradients.
Don't make generic centered layouts, simplistic gradients, and uniform styling.
Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy.
Use glass-morphism effects with backdrop filters (12-24px blur).
You MUST NOT apply universal transition. Eg: `transition: all`. This results in breaking transforms.
You MUST NOT center align the app container, ie do not add `.App { text-align: center; }` in the css file. This disrupts the human natural reading flow of text.
Always use modern button styles like pill-shaped, or sharp button with interaction animations & color relevant to the app style.
NEVER: use AI assistant Emoji characters like 🤖🧠💭💡🔮🎯📚🎭🎬🎪🎉🎊🎁🎀🎂🍰🎈🎨🎰💰💵💳🏦💎🪙💸🤑📊📈📉💹🔢🏆🥇 etc for icons.
Every interaction needs micro-animations - hover states, transitions, parallax effects, and entrance animations.
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable. Cramped designs look cheap.
Subtle grain textures, noise overlays, custom cursors, selection states, and loading animations: separates good from extraordinary.
Prioritize using pre-existing components from src/components/ui when applicable.
Components MUST use named exports (`export const ComponentName = ...`)
Pages MUST use default exports (`export default function PageName() {...}`)
